entries.claim: Pairing payloads derive the same cross-device session key on paired devices, reject weak or mismatched KDF profiles, and cannot be opened with a device-local-only key.
entries.evidence: Session crypto tests passed, including Argon2id/HKDF pairing-key derivation, weak-KDF rejection, envelope/profile mismatch rejection, downgrade resistance, and device-local key rejection.

entries.claim: Stored turns and offline pending writes are sealed ciphertext, durable across restart, owner-only on Unix, byte-idempotent during sync, and report forks instead of silently choosing a divergent head.
entries.evidence: Session tests passed, including only_ciphertext_is_stored_no_plaintext, filesystem_cache_persists_offline_pending_restart, file_session_cache_pending_files_are_owner_only, sync_pending_removes_files_after_success_and_is_idempotent, sync_pending_remote_different_bytes_errors_and_keeps_pending, load_reports_two_heads_as_fork, and forced_offline_concurrent_turns_surface_fork_on_reconnect.

entries.claim: The OneDrive transport enforces create-if-absent turn writes, byte-idempotent existing-turn handling, an exclusive active-turn lease, If-Match takeover, holder/lease-id checked renewal, and holder/lease-id checked release semantics.
entries.evidence: Graph transport tests passed, including graph_turn_put_conflict_same_bytes_is_idempotent, graph_turn_put_conflict_different_bytes_is_error, graph_lease_create_conflict_returns_busy, graph_lease_takeover_uses_if_match, graph_lease_takeover_lost_race_does_not_acquire, graph_lease_renew_checks_holder_and_lease_id, and graph_lease_release_checks_holder_and_lease_id.

entries.claim: The Android JNI-only KDF benchmark path is feature-gated and produces redacted structured Pixel 8 Pro Argon2id/HKDF timing evidence.
entries.evidence: Pixel 8 Pro JNI benchmark recorded median_ms=213.001 and p95_ms=218.6 for Argon2id/HKDF with memory_kib=65536, iterations=3, lanes=4.
entries.notes: Feature-gated JNI benchmark only; #620 still owns Android Keystore persistence.

entries.claim: A live OneDrive session roundtrip stores ciphertext-only turn files, blocks a second active lease holder, reports a forced offline fork, and deletes the fixture during cleanup.
entries.evidence: Live OneDrive evidence recorded ciphertext_only=true, lease_blocked_second_holder=true, fork_reported=true with two fork heads, turn_count=5, and cleanup_verify=DELETED status=404.
entries.notes: The test used a fresh M365 write token from the local MSAL cache without printing the token.

entries.claim: The #619 branch is green under the planned host gates for formatting, clippy, agent tests, graph tests, traceability, evidence validation, and whitespace diff checks.
entries.command: cargo +1.95.0 fmt --all --check && cargo +1.95.0 clippy -p isyncyou-agent --all-targets --features onedrive,retrieval -- -D warnings && cargo +1.95.0 clippy -p isyncyou-graph --all-targets --features http -- -D warnings && cargo +1.95.0 test -p isyncyou-agent --features onedrive,retrieval -- --nocapture && cargo +1.95.0 test -p isyncyou-graph --features http -- --nocapture && python3 tools/check_traceability.py && python3 tools/check_evidence.py --manifest docs/evidence/issue-619-manifest.json && git diff --check origin/dev...HEAD
entries.evidence: fmt passed; agent clippy passed; graph clippy passed; agent tests passed 123/123 with the live OneDrive integration test ignored in the non-live gate; graph tests passed 108/108 with 7 live tests ignored; traceability passed; evidence manifest passed; git diff --check passed.
